message: fix: allow legacy JSON without directed flag (franken_networkx-c0w6)

@@ -138,6 +138,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-bvz","title":"Review dispatch risk_probability hardcoded values","description":"## What

The fnx-dispatch and fnx-readwrite crates pass hardcoded risk_probability values
in DispatchRequest structs throughout the code. Examples:

- fnx-readwrite: risk_probability: 0.03 (edgelist write), 0.09 (json read)
- fnx-dispatch test: risk_probability: 0.01, 0.2, 0.3

These values feed into the decision_theoretic_action() function which determines
whether an operation is allowed in strict vs hardened mode.

## Assessment

The risk_probability values are NOT arbitrary magic numbers — they represent
calibrated estimates of the probability that an operation introduces
incompatibility with NetworkX. They are:

- 0.01-0.03: well-tested, low-risk operations (edgelist write)
- 0.08-0.09: moderate operations with known edge cases (json parse, generators)
- 0.2-0.5: higher-risk operations (format conversion, dispatch routing)

## What To Consider

1. Should these be configurable per-mode? (strict might want lower thresholds)
2. Should they be documented as calibration parameters?
3. Are the current values justified by evidence? (conformance test coverage)
4. The test at line 354 uses 0.2 then was changed to 0.3 — why?

## Recommended Action

Add a comment block at the top of fnx-readwrite documenting the risk calibration
methodology. Don't change the values unless conformance evidence supports it.

## Files To Review

- crates/fnx-readwrite/src/lib.rs (all DispatchRequest sites)
- crates/fnx-dispatch/src/lib.rs (decision_theoretic_action thresholds)
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs (generator risk_probability values)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:55.645980347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:23:34.406675540Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:23:34.406070695Z","close_reason":"Added calibration documentation at top of fnx-readwrite/src/lib.rs explaining all risk_probability tiers (0.03/0.08/0.09) with justification. Values are correct and calibrated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-ciq6","title":"SHIP: Publish to PyPI with pre-built wheels for Linux/macOS/Windows","description":"## Status
release.yml is ALREADY BUILT by GoldTiger — comprehensive wheel building for Linux (x86_64+aarch64), musllinux, macOS (x86_64+arm64), Windows, plus sdist. Includes cross-platform pytest verification and PyPI trusted publishing.

## Remaining work (operational, not code)
1. Configure PyPI trusted publisher in PyPI account settings
   - Go to pypi.org → Manage → Publishing → Add trusted publisher
   - Set: repository=Dicklesworthstone/franken_networkx, workflow=release.yml, environment=pypi
2. Verify version consistency: pyproject.toml version matches Cargo.toml workspace version
3. Create first release tag: git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0
4. Monitor GitHub Actions release workflow
5. Verify: pip install franken-networkx works on clean venv

## Testing
After first publish:
- pip install franken-networkx in fresh venv
- import franken_networkx as fnx
- fnx.shortest_path(fnx.path_graph(5), 0, 4)
- Verify on Linux, macOS, Windows

## Acceptance criteria
- pip install franken-networkx works from PyPI
- Wheels available for 6 platform targets
- pytest passes on installed wheel (not editable)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:48:46.342179872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:31.693561354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T18:50:31.693375272Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-ciq6","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-zrlb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:45.378594684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
